memberdef.argsstring: )(struct bt_conn *conn, const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_member *member, int err, size_t set_count)
memberdef.name: bt_csip_set_coordinator_discover_cb

briefdescription.para: Callback for discovering Coordinated Set Identification Services.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the remote device.
Pointer to the set member.
0 on success, or an errno value on error.
Number of sets on the member.

briefdescription.para: Callback for locking a set across one or more devices.

parameterdescription.para: 0 on success, or an errno value on error.

briefdescription.para: Callback when the lock value on a set of a connected device changes.

parameterdescription.para: The Coordinated Set Identification Service instance that was changed.
Whether the lock is locked or release.

simplesect.para: int Return 0 on success, or an errno value on error.

briefdescription.para: Callback when the SIRK value of a set of a connected device changes.

parameterdescription.para: The Coordinated Set Identification Service instance that was changed. The new SIRK can be accessed via the inst.info.

briefdescription.para: Callback when the size of a set of a connected device changes.

detaileddescription.para: Since all devices in a set shall have the same set size value. Each connected device may send the same new size set in a notification, assuming that the remote device supports notifications of the set size.

The rank of each device in the set may also change as part of this, so it is advisable to call bt_csip_set_coordinator_discover() to rediscover and read the characteristic values of the sets on each device.

parameterdescription.para: The Coordinated Set Identification Service instance that was changed. The new size is stored in the inst->info.size.

memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_info *set_info, int err, bool locked, struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_member *member)
memberdef.name: bt_csip_set_coordinator_ordered_access_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Callback for bt_csip_set_coordinator_ordered_access()

detaileddescription.para: If any of the set members supplied to bt_csip_set_coordinator_ordered_access() is in the locked state, this will be called with locked true and member will be the locked member, and the ordered access procedure is cancelled. Likewise, if any error occurs, the procedure will also be aborted.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the a specific set_info struct.
Error value. 0 on success, GATT error or errno on fail.
Whether the lock is locked or release.
The locked member if locked is true, otherwise NULL.

memberdef.definition: typedef bool(* bt_csip_set_coordinator_ordered_access_t) (const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_info *set_info, struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_member *members[], size_t count)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_info *set_info, struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_member *members[], size_t count)
memberdef.name: bt_csip_set_coordinator_ordered_access_t

briefdescription.para: Callback function definition for bt_csip_set_coordinator_ordered_access()

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the a specific set_info struct.
Array of members ordered by rank. The procedure shall be done on the members in ascending order.
Number of members in members.

simplesect.para: true if the procedures can be successfully done, or false to stop the procedure.

briefdescription.para: Get the service declaration attribute.

detaileddescription.para: The first service attribute can be included in any other GATT service.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the Coordinated Set Identification Service.

briefdescription.para: Register a Coordinated Set Identification Service instance.

detaileddescription.para: This will register and enable the service and make it discoverable by clients.
This shall only be done as a server.

parameterdescription.para: Coordinated Set Identification Service register parameters.
Pointer to the registered Coordinated Set Identification Service.

briefdescription.para: Unregister a Coordinated Set Identification Service instance.

detaileddescription.para: This will unregister and disable the service instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the registered Coordinated Set Identification Service.

briefdescription.para: Set the SIRK of a service instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the registered Coordinated Set Identification Service.
The new SIRK.

briefdescription.para: Set a new size and rank for a service instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be used to dynamically change the size and rank of a service instance. It is important to note that a set cannot have multiple devices with the same rank in a set, and it is up to the caller of this function to ensure that. Similarly, it is important that the size is updated on all devices in the set at the same time. The rank of a device cannot be modified on its own, and a new rank can only be set if the size is different from the current set size.
If CONFIG_BT_CSIP_SET_MEMBER_SIZE_NOTIFIABLE is enabled, this will also send a notification to all connected or bonded clients.

parameterdescription.para: The service instance.
The new set size.
The new rank. Ignored if the svc_inst is not lockable.
svc_inst is NULL, size is less than 1, rank is less than 1 or higher than size for a lockable svc_inst.
size is already set.
Success.

briefdescription.para: Get information about a service instances.

parameterdescription.para: The service instance.
Pointer to a struct to store the information in.
svc_inst or info is NULL.
Success.

briefdescription.para: Generate the Resolvable Set Identifier (RSI) value.

detaileddescription.para: This will generate RSI for given svc_inst instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the Coordinated Set Identification Service.
Pointer to the 6-octet newly generated RSI data in little-endian.

simplesect.para: int 0 if on success, errno on error.

briefdescription.para: Locks a specific Coordinated Set Identification Service instance on the server.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the Coordinated Set Identification Service.
If true lock the set, if false release the set.
This argument only have meaning when lock is false (release) and will force release the lock, regardless of who took the lock.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, GATT error on error.

briefdescription.para: Initialise the csip_set_coordinator instance for a connection.

detaileddescription.para: This will do a discovery on the device and prepare the instance for following commands.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to remote device to perform discovery on.

simplesect.para: int Return 0 on success, or an errno value on error.

briefdescription.para: Get the set member from a connection pointer.

detaileddescription.para: Get the Coordinated Set Identification Profile Set Coordinator pointer from a connection pointer. Only Set Coordinators that have been initiated via bt_csip_set_coordinator_discover() can be retrieved.

parameterdescription.para: Connection pointer.
to a Coordinated Set Identification Profile Set Coordinator instance
if conn is NULL or if the connection has not done discovery yet

briefdescription.para: Check if advertising data indicates a set member.

parameterdescription.para: The SIRK of the set to check against
The advertising data

simplesect.para: true if the advertising data indicates a set member, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Registers callbacks for csip_set_coordinator.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the callback structure.

simplesect.para: Return 0 on success, or an errno value on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_member *members[], uint8_t count, const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_info *set_info, bt_csip_set_coordinator_ordered_access_t cb)
memberdef.name: bt_csip_set_coordinator_ordered_access

briefdescription.para: Access Coordinated Set devices in an ordered manner as a client.

detaileddescription.para: This function will read the lock state of all devices and if all devices are in the unlocked state, then cb will be called with the same members as provided by members, but where the members are ordered by rank (if present). Once this procedure is finished or an error occurs, bt_csip_set_coordinator_cb::ordered_access will be called.
This procedure only works if all the members have the lock characteristic, and all either has rank = 0 or unique ranks.
If any of the members are in the locked state, the procedure will be cancelled.
This can only be done on members that are bonded.

parameterdescription.para: Array of set members to access.
Number of set members in members.
Pointer to the a specific set_info struct, as a member may be part of multiple sets.
The callback function to be called for each member.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_member **members, uint8_t count, const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_info *set_info)
memberdef.name: bt_csip_set_coordinator_lock

briefdescription.para: Lock an array of set members.

detaileddescription.para: The members will be locked starting from lowest rank going up.

Available only when the following Kconfig options are enabled: CONFIG_BT_CSIP_SET_COORDINATOR, CONFIG_BT_BONDABLE.
TODO: If locking fails, the already locked members will not be unlocked.

parameterdescription.para: Array of set members to lock.
Number of set members in members.
Pointer to the a specific set_info struct, as a member may be part of multiple sets.

simplesect.para: Return 0 on success, or an errno value on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_member **members, uint8_t count, const struct bt_csip_set_coordinator_set_info *set_info)
memberdef.name: bt_csip_set_coordinator_release

briefdescription.para: Release an array of set members.

detaileddescription.para: The members will be released starting from highest rank going down.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig options are enabled: CONFIG_BT_CSIP_SET_COORDINATOR, CONFIG_BT_BONDABLE.

parameterdescription.para: Array of set members to lock.
Number of set members in members.
Pointer to the a specific set_info struct, as a member may be part of multiple sets.

simplesect.para: Return 0 on success, or an errno value on error.

briefdescription.para: Recommended timer for member discovery.
Defines the maximum number of Coordinated Set Identification service instances for the Coordinated Set Identification Set Coordinator.
Accept the request to read the SIRK as plaintext.
Accept the request to read the SIRK, but return encrypted SIRK.
Reject the request to read the SIRK.
SIRK is available only via an OOB procedure.
Size of the Set Identification Resolving Key (SIRK)
Size of the Resolvable Set Identifier (RSI)
Service is already locked.
Service is not locked.
Invalid lock value.
SIRK only available out-of-band.
Client is already owner of the lock.
Helper to declare bt_data array including RSI.

detaileddescription.para: This macro is mainly for creating an array of struct bt_data elements which is then passed to e.g. bt_le_ext_adv_start().

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the RSI value

briefdescription.para: Coordinated Set Identification Profile (CSIP)

detaileddescription.para:
3.0
0.8.0
The Coordinated Set Identification Profile (CSIP) provides procedures to discover and coordinate sets of devices.
